Raïs M'Bolhi.bio: Veteran Algerian goalkeeper with over a century of caps, known for his heroics at the 2014 World Cup in Brazil. One of the most experienced players in Algerian football history.
Raïs M'Bolhi.playingStyle: Shot-stopper with excellent reflexes and commanding presence in the box
Raïs M'Bolhi.worldCup2026Role: Experienced third-choice goalkeeper and squad mentor providing leadership and tournament know-how
Raïs M'Bolhi.notableFact: Made crucial saves in Algeria's historic Round of 16 run at the 2014 World Cup in Brazil

Aïssa Mandi.bio: Seasoned center-back who has been a mainstay of the Algerian defense for nearly a decade. Spent years in La Liga with Real Betis before moving to Villarreal.
Aïssa Mandi.playingStyle: Ball-playing center-back comfortable building from the back with strong aerial ability
Aïssa Mandi.worldCup2026Role: Experienced defensive leader who organizes the backline and provides calm distribution
Aïssa Mandi.notableFact: Was part of the Algeria squad that won the 2019 Africa Cup of Nations in Egypt

Djamel Benlamri.bio: Tough, no-nonsense center-back who has represented Algeria at senior level for years. Moved to the Saudi Pro League after stints in the Algerian and French leagues.
Djamel Benlamri.playingStyle: Physical, aggressive defender who wins duels in the air and on the ground
Djamel Benlamri.worldCup2026Role: Veteran defensive depth option providing physicality and experience from the bench
Djamel Benlamri.notableFact: Scored in the 2019 AFCON semi-final against Nigeria to help Algeria reach the final

Youcef Atal.bio: Dynamic right-back who burst onto the international scene with electrifying performances at the 2019 Africa Cup of Nations. Known for his explosive pace and attacking runs.
Youcef Atal.playingStyle: Attack-minded full-back who bombs forward with pace and delivers dangerous crosses
Youcef Atal.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back providing width and offensive thrust down the flank
Youcef Atal.notableFact: Scored a stunning brace against Tanzania at the 2019 AFCON, showcasing his attacking threat from defense

Ramy Bensebaini.bio: Versatile left-back who has thrived at the highest level of European football with Borussia Dortmund. Equally adept at left-back and center-back with a knack for scoring goals.
Ramy Bensebaini.playingStyle: Two-way full-back combining defensive solidity with dangerous runs into the box
Ramy Bensebaini.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back and defensive anchor providing both defensive security and attacking output
Ramy Bensebaini.notableFact: Has scored key goals in the Champions League and Bundesliga from his defensive position at Borussia Dortmund

Ibrahim Maza.bio: Exciting young playmaker who chose Algeria over Germany at international level. Born in Berlin to Algerian parents, he has been turning heads with his creative ability at Hertha Berlin.
Ibrahim Maza.playingStyle: Technically gifted attacking midfielder with excellent vision and dribbling in tight spaces
Ibrahim Maza.worldCup2026Role: Young creative spark off the bench offering flair and unpredictability in the final third
Ibrahim Maza.notableFact: Born and raised in Germany, he chose to represent Algeria internationally, turning down the chance to play for the German national team

Riyad Mahrez.bio: Algeria's all-time great and former Premier League Player of the Year. Led Leicester City to their miraculous title win and won multiple trophies at Manchester City before moving to Saudi Arabia.
Riyad Mahrez.playingStyle: Silky left-footed winger who cuts inside from the right to create and score with trademark curling shots
Riyad Mahrez.worldCup2026Role: Talisman and captain figure who drives Algeria's attack with his world-class quality on the ball
Riyad Mahrez.notableFact: Won the Premier League with Leicester City in their legendary 2015-16 title-winning season and was named PFA Player of the Year

Yacine Brahimi.bio: Skillful winger who made his name at FC Porto, becoming one of the most decorated Algerian players in European football. Known for his flair and ability to beat defenders one-on-one.
Yacine Brahimi.playingStyle: Tricky winger with quick feet, excellent close control, and the ability to unlock defenses
Yacine Brahimi.worldCup2026Role: Experienced squad player providing tactical versatility and creativity from the bench
Yacine Brahimi.notableFact: Won the CAF African Footballer of the Year award and multiple Portuguese league titles with FC Porto

Islam Slimani.bio: Algeria's all-time leading goalscorer and an iconic figure in Algerian football. The towering striker has played across Europe including in the Premier League with Leicester City.
Islam Slimani.playingStyle: Target man who excels in aerial duels and hold-up play, linking midfield to attack
Islam Slimani.worldCup2026Role: Veteran striker option off the bench who can change games with his aerial presence and experience
Islam Slimani.notableFact: Algeria's all-time leading scorer with 42 international goals, making him a national football legend

Sofiane Feghouli.bio: Versatile attacker who spent his prime years in La Liga with Valencia and later played for Galatasaray. A key member of Algeria's golden generation.
Sofiane Feghouli.playingStyle: Intelligent wide player who drifts inside to link play and create scoring opportunities
Sofiane Feghouli.worldCup2026Role: Senior squad member providing depth in attack and leadership in the dressing room
Sofiane Feghouli.notableFact: Scored Algeria's goal against Belgium at the 2014 World Cup, one of the most memorable moments in Algerian football history

Saïd Benrahma.bio: Algerian-born attacker who developed in the French youth system and made his name with dazzling performances at Brentford in the Championship before moving to West Ham and then Lyon.
Saïd Benrahma.playingStyle: Tricky, left-footed attacker who loves cutting inside and unleashing shots from distance
Saïd Benrahma.worldCup2026Role: Key attacking option on the left wing providing creativity, pace, and goal threat
Saïd Benrahma.notableFact: Nicknamed 'The Smile' at Brentford for his joyful playing style, he formed a lethal attacking trio that earned promotion to the Premier League

Alexandre Oukidja.bio: French-born goalkeeper of Algerian descent who has been a reliable presence in Ligue 1 with Metz. Brings stability and consistency between the posts.
Alexandre Oukidja.playingStyle: Composed goalkeeper with good distribution and solid shot-stopping ability
Alexandre Oukidja.worldCup2026Role: Second-choice goalkeeper providing experienced backup to the starting keeper
Alexandre Oukidja.notableFact: Born in France to Algerian parents, he chose to represent Algeria at international level

Abdelkader Bedrane.bio: Dependable center-back who has been a solid performer in the Algerian domestic league with CS Constantine. Brings organizational skills and reliability to the defense.
Abdelkader Bedrane.playingStyle: Disciplined, positionally aware center-back who reads the game well
Abdelkader Bedrane.worldCup2026Role: Defensive depth option providing cover across the backline
Abdelkader Bedrane.notableFact: A consistent performer in Algerian domestic football, he has been a key part of CS Constantine's success in continental competitions

Ismaël Bennacer.bio: World-class midfielder who has been the heartbeat of AC Milan's midfield. Won the AFCON Best Player award in 2019 and is widely regarded as one of the best central midfielders in Serie A.
Ismaël Bennacer.playingStyle: Press-resistant deep-lying playmaker who controls tempo and breaks lines with incisive passing
Ismaël Bennacer.worldCup2026Role: Midfield engine and Algeria's most important player, dictating tempo and controlling possession
Ismaël Bennacer.notableFact: Named Best Player of the 2019 Africa Cup of Nations after masterful performances that powered Algeria to the title

Haris Belkebla.bio: Industrious central midfielder who has carved out a solid career in Ligue 1 with Brest. Known for his tireless work rate and ability to cover ground in midfield.
Haris Belkebla.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder with high energy, strong pressing, and solid passing
Haris Belkebla.worldCup2026Role: Midfield rotation option who can disrupt opposition play and provide energy off the bench
Haris Belkebla.notableFact: Has been a key contributor to Brest's rise in Ligue 1, helping the club punch above its weight in French football

Nabil Bentaleb.bio: Technically gifted midfielder who came through the Tottenham Hotspur academy before playing in the Bundesliga with Schalke 04 and then returning to French football.
Nabil Bentaleb.playingStyle: Composed central midfielder with excellent range of passing and ability to dictate play from deep
Nabil Bentaleb.worldCup2026Role: Experienced midfield option offering passing quality and set-piece expertise
Nabil Bentaleb.notableFact: Made his Tottenham debut at just 19 years old and became one of the youngest Algerians to play in the Premier League

Ramiz Zerrouki.bio: Dutch-born midfielder of Algerian heritage who has developed well at FC Twente in the Eredivisie. Combines physicality with technical quality in the center of the park.
Ramiz Zerrouki.playingStyle: Strong, physical midfielder who excels in duels and transitions from defense to attack
Ramiz Zerrouki.worldCup2026Role: Midfield enforcer providing physical presence and defensive solidity in the center
Ramiz Zerrouki.notableFact: Born and raised in the Netherlands, he chose to represent Algeria after being scouted by the federation

Amine Gouiri.bio: Highly talented young attacker who came through the Lyon academy alongside some of France's brightest talents. Chose Algeria over France at international level and has been a standout at Rennes.
Amine Gouiri.playingStyle: Versatile forward who can play across the front line, combining clinical finishing with creative playmaking
Amine Gouiri.worldCup2026Role: Key attacking talent who can play as a winger or second striker, providing goals and creativity
Amine Gouiri.notableFact: Turned down France to represent Algeria, having been highly coveted by both federations due to his exceptional talent

Baghdad Bounedjah.bio: Prolific striker who has been a goalscoring machine in Qatar with Al-Sadd. Scored the winning goal in the 2019 AFCON final, etching his name in Algerian football folklore.
Baghdad Bounedjah.playingStyle: Clinical penalty box striker with sharp movement and lethal finishing
Baghdad Bounedjah.worldCup2026Role: Experienced striker option providing proven goalscoring instinct and tournament pedigree
Baghdad Bounedjah.notableFact: Scored the only goal in the 2019 AFCON final against Senegal with a deflected long-range strike in the opening minutes

Farès Chaïbi.bio: Young Algerian playmaker making waves in the Bundesliga with Eintracht Frankfurt. One of the brightest emerging talents in Algerian football with exceptional technical ability.
Farès Chaïbi.playingStyle: Quick, agile attacking midfielder who threads passes through tight defenses and drives forward with the ball
Farès Chaïbi.worldCup2026Role: Emerging creative force in midfield expected to start or play a significant role off the bench
Farès Chaïbi.notableFact: Made the move from Algerian football to the Bundesliga, quickly establishing himself as a first-team regular at Eintracht Frankfurt

Adam Ounas.bio: Flair player with electric pace and dribbling ability who has played in Serie A and Ligue 1. Known for his ability to beat defenders and create something from nothing.
Adam Ounas.playingStyle: Direct, pacy winger who takes on defenders and creates chaos with his unpredictable dribbling
Adam Ounas.worldCup2026Role: Impact substitute who can change games with his pace and dribbling when introduced against tiring defenses
Adam Ounas.notableFact: Came through the Bordeaux youth system in France and has played for Napoli and Nice in addition to Lille

Mostafa Zeghba.bio: Reliable domestic goalkeeper who has been a consistent performer for ES Setif in the Algerian league. Provides solid depth to Algeria's goalkeeping department.
Mostafa Zeghba.playingStyle: Traditional shot-stopper with good positioning and reliable handling
Mostafa Zeghba.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper providing squad depth and training competition
Mostafa Zeghba.notableFact: Has been instrumental in ES Setif's domestic and continental campaigns in Algerian football

Houssem Aouar.bio: Supremely talented midfielder who rose to prominence at Lyon, starring in their run to the Champions League semi-finals in 2020. Has since moved to Saudi Arabia's Al-Ittihad.
Houssem Aouar.playingStyle: Elegant, technically brilliant midfielder who glides past opponents and picks out killer passes
keyStrengths: Ability to play between the lines
Houssem Aouar.worldCup2026Role: Creative midfielder capable of unlocking defenses with his vision and technical quality
Houssem Aouar.notableFact: Was a key player in Lyon's shock run to the 2020 Champions League semi-finals, eliminating Manchester City along the way

Mehdi Tahrat.bio: Experienced center-back who has built a solid career in Ligue 1. Provides dependable defensive cover and has been a steady presence in the Algerian squad.
Mehdi Tahrat.playingStyle: Solid, no-frills defender who focuses on defensive fundamentals and organization
Mehdi Tahrat.worldCup2026Role: Experienced defensive backup who can slot in across the backline when needed
Mehdi Tahrat.notableFact: Has spent the bulk of his career in French football, gaining valuable experience across multiple Ligue 1 clubs

Yassine Benzia.bio: Former Lyon academy product who has journeyed through several European leagues. A versatile attacker with good technical ability who now plies his trade in Turkey.
Yassine Benzia.playingStyle: Intelligent wide attacker who combines good movement with technical skill and an eye for goal
Yassine Benzia.worldCup2026Role: Squad depth option in attack providing versatility across the forward line
Yassine Benzia.notableFact: Graduated from the famed Lyon academy alongside several future French internationals before choosing to represent Algeria

Mohamed Amoura.bio: Explosive young striker who has rapidly risen to prominence with impressive goalscoring form at Wolfsburg in the Bundesliga. One of Algeria's most exciting young talents.
Mohamed Amoura.playingStyle: Rapid, direct striker who runs in behind defenses and finishes with composure beyond his years
Mohamed Amoura.worldCup2026Role: Starting striker or key attacking option who leads the line with pace and energy
Mohamed Amoura.notableFact: Burst onto the scene at Young Boys in Switzerland before earning a Bundesliga move to Wolfsburg with his prolific scoring record
